# BIRDIE

This repository contains code and documentation related to the South
Africa Biodiversity Data Pipeline for Wetlands and Waterbirds (BIRDIE)
[project](https://jrsbiodiversity.org/grants/sanbi-2020/).

The aim of the BIRDIE project is to develop a data-to-decision pipeline
for wetlands and waterbirds that uses use statistical analyses to
compute policy-relevant indicators using citizen science data. The data
pipeline initiates pulling data from different databases and
repositories, data then moves through cleaning and validation steps, and
into statistical analyses and summaries that result in indicators that
are useful for decision makers. These indicators are then post-processed
and presented on website. These indicators are useful to support South
Africa’s national and international reporting requirements, management
of wetland sites, and broader interest in wetlands and waterbirds.

This repository contains the code necessary for data acquisition and
statistical modelling, which are conducted in R. Modelling outputs are
then exported to a MySQL database that is hosted on a different system
together with the website and the API services that allow communication
between the two. However, here we will not be concerned with anything
beyond the computation of basic indicators in R.

There are two basic elements in the BIRDIE repo: i) a package that
contains functions and data that is installed as an extension to base R
and ii) an analysis folder that contains analysis scripts that run in R.
In other words, the package adds functionality to R and the analysis
scripts use this functionality. This means that to use the pipeline we
don’t only have to install the package, but we also need to clone the
repository to have access to the scripts.

Inside of the package there are some vignettes that explain how to run
the different modules of the pipeline. These vignettes are also
available as [articles](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/).

## INSTRUCTIONS TO CLONE AND INSTALL PACKAGE

1.  Clone the repository to your local machine:
    - In RStudio, create a new project
    - In the ‘Create project’ menu, select ‘Version Control’/‘Git’
    - Copy the repository URL (click on the ‘Code’ green button and copy
      the link)
    - Choose the appropiate directory and ‘Create project’
2.  Install the package ‘devtools’ in case you don´t have it and run
    `devtools::install(build_vignettes = TRUE)` from the project
    directory
3.  Remember to pull the latest version regularly

## INSTRUCTIONS TO CONTRIBUTE CODE TO THE PACKAGE

For site owners:

There is the danger of multiple people working simultaneously on the
project code. If you make changes locally on your computer and, before
you push your changes, others push theirs, there might be conflicts.
This is because the HEAD pointer in the main branch has moved since you
started working.

To deal with these lurking issues, I would suggest opening and working
on a topic branch. This is a just a regular branch that has a short
lifespan. In steps:

- Open a branch at your local machine
- Push to the remote repo
- Make your changes in your local machine
- Commit and push to remote
- Merge your changes:
  - In the GitHub repo you will now see an option that notifies of
    changes in a branch: click compare and pull request.
  - If there are no conflicts ‘merge pull request’
- Delete the branch. You will have to delete it in the remote repo
  (GitHub) and also in your local machine. In your local machine you
  have to use Git directly, apparently RStudio doesn´t do it:
  - In your local machine, change to master branch.
  - Either use the Git GUI (go to branches/delete/select branch/push).
  - Or use the console typing ‘git branch -d your_branch_name’.

Opening branches is quick and easy, so there is no harm in opening
multiple branches a day. However, it is important to merge and delete
them often to keep things tidy. Git provides functionality to deal with
conflicting branches. More about branches here:

[Git Branching in a
Nutshell](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Branching-Branches-in-a-Nutshell)

Another idea is to use the ‘issues’ tab that you find in the project
header. There, we can identify issues with the package, assign tasks and
warn other contributors that we will be working on the code.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`addDuToitCounts()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/addDuToitCounts.md)
  : Add DuToit's Pan data from Doug
- [`addExtraSitesToSummary()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/addExtraSitesToSummary.md)
  : Add raw counts to CWAC SSM predictions
- [`addSiteVarEETimeLimit()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/addSiteVarEETimeLimit.md)
  : Annotate site data with Google Earth Engine with a time limit
- [`addSpOccDetCovt()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/addSpOccDetCovt.md)
  : Add detection covariate to spOccupancy data list
- [`barberspan`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/barberspan.md)
  : Barberspan CWAC data
- [`combineAbuDiags()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/combineAbuDiags.md)
  : Combine species abundance diagnostics into a single object
- [`combineOccuDiags()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/combineOccuDiags.md)
  : Combine species occupancy diagnostics into a single object
- [`configPipeline()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/configPipeline.md)
  : Configure pipeline parameters
- [`createCombinedExportFile()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/createCombinedExportFile.md)
  : Create a combined file for export to data mart
- [`createLog()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/createLog.md)
  : Create pipeline event log
- [`createOccuData()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/createOccuData.md)
  : Create data for fitting an occupancy model
- [`createPredFromAbap()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/createPredFromAbap.md)
  : Create prediction object directly from ABAP data
- [`diagnoseGofJagsSsm()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/diagnoseGofJagsSsm.md)
  : Diagnose goodness-of-fit for JAGS state-space model
- [`diagnoseGofSpOccu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/diagnoseGofSpOccu.md)
  : Conduct goodness-of-fit test for spOccupancy models
- [`diagnoseRhatJagsSsm()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/diagnoseRhatJagsSsm.md)
  : Diagnose convergence for JAGS state-space model
- [`diagnoseRhatSpOccu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/diagnoseRhatSpOccu.md)
  : Diagnose convergence for spOccupancy model
- [`fitSpOccu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/fitSpOccu.md)
  : Fit spOccupancy model
- [`gatherYearFromVars()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/gatherYearFromVars.md)
  : Gather years from variables with year
- [`logFitStatus()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/logFitStatus.md)
  : Generate fit status messages
- [`pipe_prll_fit()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/pipe_prll_fit.md)
  : Fit occupancy models in parallel
- [`plotDetections()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/plotDetections.md)
  : Plot detection from occupancy data
- [`plotJagsSsm2ss()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/plotJagsSsm2ss.md)
  : Plot state series of a JAGS state-space model with two seasons
- [`plotOccuVars()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/plotOccuVars.md)
  : Plot occupancy covariates
- [`postPredDistJagsSsm()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/postPredDistJagsSsm.md)
  : Prepare posterior predictive distribution for JAGS state-space model
- [`ppl_create_data_ssm()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_create_data_ssm.md)
  : Prepare covariate data for abundance pipeline
- [`ppl_create_indtr_file()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_create_indtr_file.md)
  : Create indicator storage files
- [`ppl_create_site_visit()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_create_site_visit.md)
  : Create site and visit occupancy files
- [`ppl_diagnose_occu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_diagnose_occu.md)
  : Run diagnostics for occupancy model fit
- [`ppl_diagnose_ssm()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_diagnose_ssm.md)
  : Run diagnostics for a state-space model fit
- [`ppl_fit_occu_model()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_fit_occu_model.md)
  : Fit occupancy model
- [`ppl_fit_ssm_model()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_fit_ssm_model.md)
  : Fit state-space JAGS model
- [`ppl_run_pipe_abu1()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_run_pipe_abu1.md)
  : Run abundance pipeline ABU1
- [`ppl_run_pipe_dst1()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_run_pipe_dst1.md)
  : Run distribution indicators pipeline module 1
- [`ppl_summarise_occu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_summarise_occu.md)
  : Summarise predictions from occupancy model
- [`ppl_summarise_ssm()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/ppl_summarise_ssm.md)
  : Summarise predictions from state-space JAGS model
- [`predictSpOccu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/predictSpOccu.md)
  : Predict from spOccupancy model fit
- [`prepGEECatchmData()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/prepGEECatchmData.md)
  : Prepare Google Earth Engine data for catchments
- [`prepGEESiteData()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/prepGEESiteData.md)
  : Prepare Google Earth Engine site data
- [`prepGEEVisitData()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/prepGEEVisitData.md)
  : Prepare Google Earth Engine visit data
- [`prepSpOccuData_multi()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/prepSpOccuData_multi.md)
  : Prepare spOccupancy data for multi-season model fitting
- [`prepSpOccuData_single()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/prepSpOccuData_single.md)
  : Prepare spOccupancy data for single-season model fitting
- [`prepSsmData()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/prepSsmData.md)
  : Prepare CWAC data to fit a state-space model
- [`processJAGSoutput()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/processJAGSoutput.md)
  : Process JAGS model outputs
- [`scaleSpOccVars()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/scaleSpOccVars.md)
  : Scale covariates in spOccupancy-type data
- [`selectSppFromDiag()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/selectSppFromDiag.md)
  : Select species based on model diagnostics
- [`setSpOutFilePath()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/setSpOutFilePath.md)
  : Set a standard path for a pipeline output file associated with a
  species
- [`simDetSpOccu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/simDetSpOccu.md)
  : Simulate detections from spOccupancy fit
- [`summariseSpOccu()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/summariseSpOccu.md)
  : Summarise spOccupancy occupancy estimates
- [`summaryJags()`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/summaryJags.md)
  : Summarise JAGS model fit
- [`waterbirds`](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/reference/waterbirds.md)
  : BIRDIE's waterbird masterlist

### All vignettes

- [BIRDIE:
  Basics](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v01-birdie-basics.md):

  abundance](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v02-birdie-spp-abundance.md):

  distributions](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v03-birdie-spp-distributions.md):
- [BIRDIE ABU: Data
  preparation](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v04-birdie-abu-data-prep.md):
- [BIRDIE ABU: Model
  fitting](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v05-birdie-abu-model-fitting.md):
- [BIRDIE ABU: Model
  diagnostics](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v06-birdie-abu-model-diagnostics.md):
- [BIRDIE ABU: Model
  summary](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v07-birdie-abu-model-summary.md):

  preparation](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v08-birdie-dst-data-prep.md):
- [BIRDIE DST: Model

- [BIRDIE DST: Model
  summary](https://africabirddata.github.io/BIRDIE/articles/v11-birdie-dst-model-summary.md):
